formality.description: Turkish has a T-V distinction: siz (formal/plural) vs sen (informal/singular). Professional software uses siz-form. Turkish is agglutinative, so formality also appears in verb suffixes (-siniz vs -sin).

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Turkish has no grammatical gender. The third-person pronoun 'o' is gender-neutral.

formal-siz.label: Siz (Formal)
formal-siz.description: Standard for professional software, enterprise, and institutional contexts.
formal-siz.prompt: Formal Turkish. Use siz-form consistently. Professional register appropriate for software interfaces and business communication. Clear, precise vocabulary. Avoid colloquialisms.

informal-sen.label: Sen (Informal)
informal-sen.description: For consumer apps, social media, gaming, and youth-oriented products.
informal-sen.prompt: Informal Turkish. Use sen-form. Warm, conversational tone for consumer products and social platforms. Colloquial expressions acceptable.
